mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly reducing present equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show positive mean returns when FEDFUNDS increases, or Rank IC remains below 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending capacity, triggering immediate negative S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 65% of instances where UNRATE rises > 0.2pp, or signal loses statistical significance in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates synchronized consumer demand and capital investment growth, driving corporate revenue expectations higher and producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations in RSAFS and HOUST fail to predict positive 60-day returns, with interaction term coefficient statistically indistinguishable from zero in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production growth above its 12-month moving average reflects expanding manufacturing capacity and supply chain normalization, which supports corporate margins and yields positive S&P 500 forward returns over one month.
failure_condition: INDPRO growth above trend coincides with negative 20-day SPX returns in > 55% of cases, or the signal reverses direction during inflationary supply shocks.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index exceeds 300 while the federal funds rate remains below 3.0%, markets price in aggressive future tightening, causing immediate negative S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: The joint condition of high CPIAUCSL and low FEDFUNDS fails to predict negative 5-day returns, or the interaction effect vanishes when controlling for term premium movements.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in total nonfarm payrolls increases aggregate household income and consumer spending power, directly boosting corporate earnings forecasts and generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth shows no statistically significant correlation with 60-day forward returns, or Rank IC remains below 0.01 across all rolling 5-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth exceeding 2.5% annually confirms robust economic expansion and corporate revenue resilience, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month as earnings revisions turn upward.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth above 2.5% is followed by negative 20-day SPX returns in > 60% of observations, or the signal loses predictive power during stagflationary periods.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production combined with stable or declining unemployment indicates productive capacity expansion without labor market deterioration, signaling healthy economic momentum that drives positive S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: The combination of rising INDPRO and falling UNRATE fails to predict positive 5-day returns, or the interaction term becomes statistically insignificant in out-of-sample walk-forward tests.
